design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate auto-scrolling space, destroy enemies and obstacles, acquire progressive weapon upgrades (basic gun → ring → laser → missile), defeat stage boss, advance to next base
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing directional movement independence from firing direction while keeping pace with screen scroll; screen edge death creates constant spatial pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Single warrior pilot defending against galaxy-wide cyborg invasion across seven alien bases, progressively upgrading from basic armament to devastating weaponry
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: 8-directional movement decoupled from 8-directional firing creates signature control complexity; weapon upgrade progression is core progression mechanic rather than temporary power-ups; difficulty-gated secret rooms reward precision timing and exploration

difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resuming at any cleared stage
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing via password system; no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four selectable difficulty tiers (Eagle, Albatross, Ace unlocked by default; Ultimate must be unlocked); higher difficulties reduce secret room availability and increase required timing precision; path forks allow choosing harder left or easier right routes within stages

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon upgrade progression: basic cannon → ring weapon → laser weapon → missile weapon; each tier significantly more powerful; upgrades collected as drops during stages
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven sequential bases must be defeated in order; Ultimate difficulty mode requires prior completion; secret rooms accessible only with precise timing on difficulty-dependent gaps
replayability_hooks: Four difficulty settings with progressively tighter secret room timing requirements
Path forks within stages offering alternate difficulty routes
Secret rooms containing power-ups that vary by difficulty level
Password system enables challenge runs from any stage

world_building_techniques: Minimal plot framing: Burai are described as super-intelligent cyborgs bent on universal domination with seven galaxy-spanning production facilities
Player character backstory: unnamed protagonist with proton suit and laser gun as sole defender
Stage progression narrative: advancing through seven enemy bases implies dismantling invasion infrastructure
Minimal environmental storytelling; focus on mechanical progression over narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Mandatory stage-end encounters that test mastery of directional control decoupling and weapon management; bosses serve as mechanical gates between bases

faction_indicators: Burai faction: super-intelligent cyborg race with half-robot, half-animal hybrid troops
Protagonist faction: unnamed human warrior as singular defender of galaxy
danger_signals: Screen edge death mechanic creates constant threat of instant failure
Difficulty-gated secret room access signals escalating enemy capability across harder modes
Boss encounters at stage terminals represent fortified positions
environmental_storytelling: Seven distinct alien bases across galaxy communicate structured invasion network
Weapon upgrade progression (basic → ring → laser → missile) shows escalating threat level requiring superior armament
Secret room gaps reward exploration and suggest hidden supply caches within enemy territory
